prompt:
Read the workflow below and follow it. Task ID: GROVE-S03-T07.

---

## Prior phase summaries (carry-forward)

### plan
Objective: Implement grove doctor with a new core::harness module (sentinel constants + mode→content tables), core::doctor (pure diagnose with universal + explore checks), and Cmd::Doctor CLI verb with --json and exit-code semantics.
Verdict: n/a
Key changes: New core/src/harness.rs: GROVE_START, GROVE_END, MCP_SERVER_KEY, expected_mcp_args(Mode), expected_claude_marker(Mode), agents_md_expected(Mode) — resolves constant-ownership blocker.; cli/src/init.rs refactored: remove local CLAUDE_START/CLAUDE_END/MCP_SERVER_KEY consts; import from grove_core::harness — sentinel strings live in exactly one place.; New core/src/doctor.rs: Status{Ok,Warn,Fail,Info}, Check{group,name,status,detail,hint}, Report{mode,checks}, Report::ok(), diagnose(root,force)->Report — pure read-only.; core/src/lib.rs: add pub mod harness and pub mod doctor.; Universal checks: grove_version, config_present, legacy_explore_json, four harness sub-checks, registry_root, grammar_cache, project_languages, lock_integrity.; Harness sub-checks use harness::expected_mcp_args/expected_claude_marker/agents_md_expected; validate on-disk files vs declared mode; Fail on drift.; Explore-mode checks (McpLlm only): explore_config_valid, provider_reachable, model_served, allowed_tools_known, tap_config.; cli/src/main.rs: Cmd::Doctor{path,explore,standard}; dispatch arm; grouped ✓/⚠/✗ human output or --json; exit code from Report::ok().; Unit tests in core/src/doctor.rs build all fixtures by hand (tempdir + std::fs::write) — resolves test-strategy blocker (no cross-crate private helpers).; Harness drift matrix: per-mode clean fixture → all harness checks Ok; selected drift scenarios → correct Fail/Warn.; warn_only_report_exits_zero: Report::ok() true when only Warn/Info present — AC 9 warning boundary (per reviewer advisory).; Integration tests: doctor --help, clean project exits 0, --json parses, induced drift exits 1 under both output modes.
Full artifact: PLAN.md
### review_plan
Objective: Re-reviewed the revised grove doctor plan (core::harness + core::doctor + Cmd::Doctor) to confirm the two prior blockers and one advisory were resolved and all reused seams exist.
Verdict: approved
Key changes: Blocker 1 (constant ownership) resolved: new core/src/harness.rs is single source of truth; init.rs migrates to import it — no core->cli cycle, no duplication.; Verified on-disk init.rs owns CLAUDE_START/END (l.20-21) and MCP_SERVER_KEY (l.79); plan's per-mode markers match real steering blocks (outline/skill/explore).; Blocker 2 (cross-crate private test helpers) resolved: unit tests build tempdir fixtures via std::fs::write, no reconcile_harness/seed_lock imports.; Advisory (warn-only exits zero) incorporated as warn_only_report_exits_zero; Report::ok() correctly keys only off Status::Fail.; Seam active_mode(root, ModeChoice)->Mode confirmed (config.rs:291); ModeChoice None/ForceExplore/ForceStandard matches --explore/--standard precedence.; verify_lock (registry.rs:465), health_probe + HealthError{Unreachable,ModelMissing} (client.rs), list_models, ExploreConfig::validate, toolset consts, registry helpers all present.; Mode enum {Mcp,Skill,Both,McpLlm,Grammars} matches expected_mcp_args mapping; global --json flag present (main.rs:24) so AC6 dual-format exit code is implementable.; Advisory (non-blocking): harness_serve_surface is Info-only; acceptable since surface is derived and .mcp.json drift is already a hard Fail. No live determine_surface seam — compute inline.; Advisory (non-blocking): ensure explore/standard bool->ModeChoice conversion mirrors serve; decide+test precedence when both flags passed.
Full artifact: PLAN_REVIEW.md
### implementation
Objective: Implement grove doctor with core::harness (sentinel constants), core::doctor (pure diagnose), and Cmd::Doctor CLI verb with --json and exit-code semantics.
Verdict: n/a
Key changes: New core/src/harness.rs: GROVE_START, GROVE_END, MCP_SERVER_KEY, expected_mcp_args(Mode), expected_claude_marker(Mode), agents_md_expected(Mode) — single source of truth.; cli/src/init.rs: removed local CLAUDE_START/CLAUDE_END/MCP_SERVER_KEY consts; imported from grove_core::harness — sentinel strings in exactly one place.; New core/src/doctor.rs: Status{Ok,Warn,Fail,Info}, Check, Report, Report::ok(), diagnose(root,force)->Report — pure read-only using core primitives.; core/src/lib.rs: added pub mod harness and pub mod doctor (alphabetically ordered).; Universal checks (11): grove_version, config_present, legacy_explore_json, four harness sub-checks, registry_root, grammar_cache, project_languages, lock_integrity.; Harness sub-checks compare on-disk state to harness::expected_* functions; Fail on drift with grove init --as <mode> hint.; Explore-mode checks (McpLlm only): explore_config_valid, provider_reachable, model_served, allowed_tools_known, tap_config.; cli/src/main.rs: Cmd::Doctor{path,explore,standard}; grouped human output or --json; exit 1 from Report::ok() under both formats.; Unit tests in harness.rs: 3 coverage tests for all Mode variants across all three functions.; Unit tests in doctor.rs: warn_only exits zero, harness matrix all 5 modes, 4 drift scenarios, lock absent, explore absent, provider unreachable.; Integration tests in cli/tests/cli.rs: doctor --help, clean project exits 0, --json valid, drift exits nonzero (human + json).
Full artifact: PROGRESS.md
### code_review
Objective: Independently reviewed the grove doctor implementation (core::harness, core::doctor, Cmd::Doctor) against the approved PLAN and task ACs.
Verdict: approved
Key changes: Isolated true T07 footprint via git diff HEAD: reconcile_harness in the cumulative diff is committed T04 work, not T07.; T07 changes: new harness.rs + doctor.rs, lib.rs module wiring, init.rs constant migration (8 lines, aliased GROVE_START/END), main.rs Cmd::Doctor, cli.rs integration tests.; Verified independently: cargo clippy -D warnings clean; cargo test --release --locked green (177 core + 92 CLI unit + 33 integration + 1 doc, 0 failed).; Read actual harness matrix/drift/explore tests — assertions discriminate real Ok/Warn/Fail, not tautologies.; AC1 shapes exact; Report.mode is core declared Mode not Surface; diagnose pure/read-only.; AC2 mode resolution via shared active_mode(root, force); --explore/--standard precedence correct.; AC3 universal checks present; harness drift -> Fail naming the file with grove init --as <mode> hint.; AC4 lock_integrity delegates to T06 verify_lock: Mismatch->Fail, Missing/absent->Warn (never Fail).; AC5 explore checks gated on McpLlm; HealthError hints surfaced verbatim; allowed_tools warn on unknown.; AC6 Cmd::Doctor + --json dual-format with exit code from Report::ok(); both formats integration-tested.; AC8 build/clippy clean, --help documents doctor, both new files newline-terminated.; Advisory only: Report.mode shows declared (not forced) mode in header; steering legality covered via validate(); cosmetic double blank line in init.rs.
Full artifact: CODE_REVIEW.md
### validation
Objective: Validate all 10 acceptance criteria for grove doctor: core::harness sentinel constants, core::doctor types/diagnose, universal checks, harness drift detection, lock_integrity, explore-mode checks, Cmd::Doctor CLI verb with --json and exit-code semantics.
Verdict: approved
Key changes: AC1 PASS: core/src/harness.rs is single source of truth; cli/src/init.rs imports via grove_core::harness alias — no local duplicates.; AC2 PASS: core::doctor exports Status, Check, Report, diagnose — pure read-only verified by source inspection.; AC3 PASS: Doctor dispatch converts --explore/--standard to ModeChoice; diagnose calls active_mode(root,force) internally.; AC4 PASS: All 11 universal checks present: grove_version, config_present, legacy_explore_json, 4 harness sub-checks, registry_root, grammar_cache, project_languages, lock_integrity.; AC5 PASS: Harness checks Fail on drift with 'grove init --as <mode>' hint; 4 drift unit tests validate specific mismatch scenarios.; AC6 PASS: lock_integrity via verify_lock; absent→Warn (not Fail); Mismatch→Fail; Missing→Warn; unit test confirms boundary.; AC7 PASS: explore_checks gated on Mode::McpLlm; all 5 explore checks present; HealthError hints surfaced verbatim.; AC8 PASS: Cmd::Doctor + global --json + exit 1 from std::process::exit(1); 4 integration tests confirm both output formats.; AC9 PASS: Report::ok() is all(!Fail); warn_only_report_exits_zero unit test directly validates Warn/Info exits 0.; AC10 PASS: clippy clean; 303 tests (177+92+33+1), 0 failed; doctor_help_documents_verb confirms --help coverage.

# Approve Task
## Iron Laws

- Approve only when the implementation is consistent with the project's architecture and the deployment posture is understood. Architectural sign-off is not a rubber stamp — it is the last point at which cross-cutting concerns can be caught cheaply.
- Read `.forge/personas/architect.md` first; print the persona identity line (emoji, name, tagline) to stdout before any other tool use.
- All store I/O via `forge_store` (or `node .forge/tools/store-cli.cjs`). Never edit `.forge/store/*.json` directly.

## Store-Write Verification

<!-- See _fragments/store-write-verification.md for the canonical block content -->

## Algorithm

```

0a. Pre-flight Gate Check:
   - **Entity-mode resolution:** read the kickoff arguments. `--task {id}` → `entity_kind = "task"`, `record_id = {id}`. `--bug {id}` → `entity_kind = "bug"`, `record_id = {id}`. All store-cli calls below substitute `{entity_kind}` and `{record_id}` for the literal "task"/{taskId} placeholders.
   - Run: `node .forge/tools/preflight-gate.cjs --phase approve --{entity_kind} {record_id}`
   - Exit 1 (gate failed) → print stderr and HALT. Do not proceed; do not attempt to produce the artifact.
   - Exit 2 (misconfiguration) → print stderr and HALT.
   - Exit 0 → continue.

0b. Pipeline Step Guard (user-invoked state check):
   - If `--force` is present in the invocation arguments, skip this step entirely.
   - If `entity_kind == "bug"`, skip this step entirely (bug state is managed by meta-fix-bug.md).
   - Read current task state:
     `node .forge/tools/store-cli.cjs read task {record_id} --fields status`
   - Extract the `status` field from the JSON output.
   - Allowed states for this phase: `review-approved`.
   - If the current status is NOT in the allowed set:
     Print the following and HALT (do not proceed):
     `× Task {record_id} is in state '{status}' — /forge:review-code (or /forge:validate) must complete first. To run the full pipeline: /forge:run-task {record_id}`

1. Load Context:
   - Read task prompt
   - Read final PLAN.md
   - Read approved CODE_REVIEW.md
   - Read PROGRESS.md

2. Architectural Review:
   - Verify implementation aligns with project architecture
   - Check for cross-cutting concerns (impact on other modules)
   - Assess operational impact (deployment changes, migrations)

3. Sign Off:
   - Write the architect approval via:
     `forge_artifact({ command:"write", entity:"{entity_kind}", entityId:"{record_id}", artifact:"architect-approval", content:"<markdown>" })`
     The markdown content must contain:
     - A canonical verdict line for human readers, on its own line, in this exact form:
       ```
       **Verdict:** [Approved | Revision Required]
       ```
     - Approval status rationale
     - Deployment notes
     - Follow-up items for future sprints
   - The downstream commit-phase preflight gate does NOT read this markdown. **Task mode:** it reads `task.status === "approved"` set in step 4. **Bug mode:** it reads `bug.summaries.approve.verdict === "approved"` set in step 5. The `**Verdict:**` line is a human breadcrumb only.

4. Finalize:
   - Transitions:
     - **Task mode** — Update status: `node .forge/tools/store-cli.cjs update-status task {taskId} status approved`. The status IS the verdict signal for task-mode commit gate (`STATUS_SOURCE` in `read-verdict.cjs`).
     - **Bug mode** — NO status write. The bug remains `in-progress`. The verdict signal travels through `summaries.approve.verdict` written in step 5 below (read by `read-verdict.cjs § BUG_PHASE_VERDICT_SOURCE`). Writing `bug.status` here — especially writing `approved` or `verified` — violates `meta-fix-bug.md § Iron Laws #2` and is the trap that produced the FORGE-BUG-002 regression.
   - **Do NOT emit a phase event yourself.** The orchestrator (or kickoff handler) owns event emission — it composes the canonical event from runtime telemetry (model, provider, tokens, wall times) plus the SUMMARY you write in the next step. Subagents that call `store-cli emit` for phase events hallucinate runtime facts (see Plan 11 / Slice 2). Write the SUMMARY and return.

5. Emit Summary Sidecar:
   - Write the approve summary via:
     `forge_artifact({ command:"write", entity:"{entity_kind}", entityId:"{record_id}", artifact:"approve-summary", content:"<JSON>" })`
     The JSON content must have the following shape:
     ```json
     {
       "objective":   "<one sentence — what this approval covered>",
       "findings":    ["<up to 12 bullets, 200 chars each — architectural notes, deployment concerns>"],
       "verdict":     "<approved | revision>",
       "written_at":  "<current ISO 8601 timestamp>",
       "artifact_ref":"ARCHITECT_APPROVAL.md"
     }
     ```
   - Call (task mode) — optional for tasks, since `task.status` is the canonical signal.
     The sidecar path is auto-resolved from the record's `path` — never pass it:
     ```
     node .forge/tools/store-cli.cjs set-summary {taskId} approve
     ```
     Or (bug mode) — REQUIRED for bugs, this is the canonical verdict signal:
     ```
     node .forge/tools/store-cli.cjs set-bug-summary {bugId} approve
     ```
   - In bug mode, if the set-bug-summary call exits non-zero, fix the sidecar JSON and retry. Do not return without a valid summary — the downstream commit gate has no other way to read the approval verdict.
```

🗻 **grove Architect** — I hold the shape of the whole. I give final sign-off before commit.

## Identity

I am the grove Architect. I plan sprints, approve completed tasks, and maintain architectural coherence across the project. I have final sign-off before code is committed.

Run this command using the Bash tool as my first action (before any file reads or other tool use):
```bash
node .forge/tools/banners.cjs north
```
## What I Need to Know

- The full architecture of the project
- The business domain and entity model
- The current sprint's goals and priorities
- Historical complexity patterns from previous sprints
- Cross-cutting concerns and technical debt

## What I Produce

- Sprint manifests — task breakdown with dependencies, estimates, priorities
- `ARCHITECT_APPROVAL.md` — final sign-off on completed tasks
- Architecture decisions and updates to knowledge base

## Capabilities

- Plan sprints with dependency graphs
- Approve or reject completed tasks
- Update architecture documentation
- Identify cross-task conflicts and dependencies

- **Entity model and service boundaries**: See `engineering/architecture/` and `engineering/architecture/../business-domain/entity-model.md`
- **ID format and prefix convention**: Task IDs use the GROVE prefix from `.forge/config.json`
- **Technical debt areas**: 
- **Deployment topology**: See `engineering/architecture/deployment.md`
- **Operational impact categories**:

- **Syntax check**: `cargo test --release --locked`
- **Lint**: `cargo clippy -- -D warnings`

# Stack

## Language & Runtime

| Component | Technology | Version |
|-----------|-----------|---------|
| Language | Rust | 2021 edition |
| WASM runtime | wasmtime (via tree-sitter wasm feature) | bundled with tree-sitter 0.25 |
| Package manager | cargo | latest stable |

## Core Dependencies

| Crate | Version | Purpose |
|-------|---------|---------|
| tree-sitter | 0.25 (wasm feature) | Parse engine — loads grammars as WASM modules at runtime |
| clap | 4 (derive feature) | CLI argument parsing |
| serde / serde_json | 1 | JSON serialization for MCP and CLI output |
| ureq | 2 (rustls) | HTTP client for `grove fetch` (grammar downloads) |
| ignore | 0.4 | Gitignore-aware directory walking (same crate ripgrep uses) |
| sha2 | 0.10 | Content hashing for lockfile integrity |
| anyhow | 1 | Error handling |
| dirs | 5 | OS-native cache/config directory resolution |
| streaming-iterator | 0.1 | tree-sitter query cursor iteration |

## Build Profile

```toml
[profile.release]
lto = "thin"
strip = true
```

Single-binary distribution: `cargo build --release --locked` produces one static binary with no runtime dependencies.

## Test Framework

- **cargo test** — `cargo test --release --locked`
- Integration tests live in `tests/cli.rs` (374 lines of CLI smoke tests)
- Unit tests are inline in source modules (`#[cfg(test)] mod tests`)

## Lint / Type Check

- **clippy** — `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` (warnings treated as errors)

## Distribution Channels

| Channel | Mechanism |
|---------|-----------|
| GitHub Releases | 5 cross-compiled binaries (linux x86_64, linux aarch64, macos x86_64, macos aarch64, windows x86_64) |
| npm | `@entelligentsia/grove` — thin wrapper that downloads the platform binary on `npm install` |
| Homebrew | Custom formula in `dist/homebrew/grove.rb` |
| Install script | Direct binary download via GitHub Releases |

## Grammar System

Grammars are **not compiled into the binary**. They are:
1. Fetched at runtime as WASM modules via `grove fetch`
2. Cached in OS-native cache directories (`~/.cache/grove/grammars` on Linux)
3. Pinned by `grove.lock` (content-addressed, sha256 integrity)

The registry ships 3 grammars in-source: JavaScript, Python, Rust.

## MCP Server

The `grove serve` command runs an MCP server over **stdio** (JSON-RPC 2.0, newline-delimited). The protocol is implemented by hand — no async runtime, no external MCP SDK. Compatible with protocol versions `2025-06-18`, `2025-03-26`, `2024-11-05`.
Let me read the full task prompt sections for the ACs and operational impact, plus the code review verdict details.

## Operational Impact

- **Version bump:** required at release (new user-facing verb).
- **Regeneration:** none; additive command.
- **Security scan:** not required.

## Advisory notes (non-blocking)
1. `Report.mode` reports the *declared* config mode while harness checks run
   against `active_mode(root, force)`. Running `grove doctor --explore` on an
   `mcp`-declared project shows `mode: Mcp` in the header but validates harness
   against `McpLlm`. This matches AC1's literal wording ("the core declared
   Mode"), so it is correct-as-specified — but a future polish could echo the
   forced/effective mode in the human header to avoid confusion.
2. AC5's "`steering` legal" is covered transitively by `ExploreConfig::validate()`
   rather than a dedicated named check. Acceptable; flagging only for traceability.
3. Cosmetic: removing the `MCP_SERVER_KEY` const from `init.rs` left a
   double blank line before `pub fn run`. Trivial; rustfmt-neutral.

arguments.content:
# ARCHITECT_APPROVAL — GROVE-S03-T07: `grove doctor`

**Verdict:** Approved

## Approval Rationale

The implementation is architecturally coherent with the grove single-binary,
read-only-diagnostic posture. Key alignments:

- **Constant-ownership resolved cleanly.** The new `core/src/harness.rs` becomes
  the single source of truth for sentinel strings (`GROVE_START/END`,
  `MCP_SERVER_KEY`) and mode→content tables. `cli/src/init.rs` migrates to import
  them via `grove_core::harness` — no core→cli dependency cycle, no duplication.
  This is the correct layering: shared invariants live in the core crate,
  consumed by the CLI.
- **`core::doctor::diagnose` is pure and read-only.** It composes existing core
  primitives (`active_mode`, `verify_lock` from T06, `ExploreConfig::validate`,
  health probes) rather than re-implementing them. Diagnostics never mutate — the
  right contract for a `doctor` verb.
- **Reuse over reinvention.** lock_integrity delegates to T06's `verify_lock`;
  explore checks gate on `Mode::McpLlm` and surface `HealthError` hints verbatim.
  No shadow logic that could drift from the real init/serve behavior.
- **Exit-code + `--json` dual-format** keyed off `Report::ok()` (all `!Fail`) gives
  a stable CI-consumable contract; the warn-only-exits-zero boundary is explicitly
  tested.

Independent code review isolated the true T07 footprint via `git diff HEAD`
(distinguishing it from committed T04 `reconcile_harness` work), verified clippy
`-D warnings` clean and the full test suite green (303 tests, 0 failed), and
confirmed the harness/drift/explore tests discriminate real Ok/Warn/Fail rather
than tautologies. Validation independently confirmed all 10 ACs.

## Deployment Notes

- **Version bump required at release** — this adds a new user-facing verb
  (`grove doctor`). Per the task's Operational Impact, this is the only release
  gate.
- **No regeneration, no migration, no security scan** — the command is purely
  additive and read-only. It touches no persisted state, no lockfile writes, no
  network mutation. Zero operational risk to existing deployments.
- Single-binary distribution model is unaffected: the verb ships inside the same
  static binary across all five release targets.

## Follow-up Items (future sprints)

1. **Effective-mode echo in human header** (advisory #1): `Report.mode` shows the
   *declared* config mode while harness checks validate against
   `active_mode(root, force)`. Correct-as-specified for AC1, but a future polish
   could echo the forced/effective mode when `--explore`/`--standard` override the
   declared mode, to avoid operator confusion.
2. **Named steering-legality check** (advisory #2): AC5's "steering legal" is
   covered transitively by `ExploreConfig::validate()`. A dedicated named check
   would improve traceability of doctor output, but is not required.
3. **Cosmetic**: double blank line left in `init.rs` after removing the local
   `MCP_SERVER_KEY` const (rustfmt-neutral; clean up opportunistically).

None of these block commit.

content.text: Wrote 1851 bytes to engineering/sprints/GROVE-S03/GROVE-S03-T07/APPROVE-SUMMARY.json

Both artifacts written and the task transitioned to `approved`. Task-mode approve is complete.

## Architect Approval — GROVE-S03-T07 `grove doctor`

**Verdict: Approved** ✓

**Architectural assessment:**
- **Clean layering** — the constant-ownership blocker was resolved correctly: `core/src/harness.rs` is now the single source of truth for sentinel strings and mode→content tables, consumed by `cli/src/init.rs` via import. No core→cli cycle, no duplication.
- **Correct doctor contract** — `core::doctor::diagnose` is pure and read-only, composing existing core primitives (`active_mode`, T06's `verify_lock`, `ExploreConfig::validate`, health probes) rather than reimplementing them. No shadow logic that could drift from real init/serve behavior.
- **Stable CI contract** — `--json` + exit code keyed off `Report::ok()` with the warn-only-exits-zero boundary explicitly tested.

**Cross-cutting/operational:** Purely additive, read-only verb inside the same single static binary — zero risk to existing deployments. Only release gate is a **version bump** (new user-facing verb); no regeneration, migration, or security scan.

**Follow-ups (all non-blocking):** effective-mode echo in the human header, a dedicated named steering-legality check for traceability, and a cosmetic double-blank-line cleanup in `init.rs`.

Status: `review-approved → approved`. Ready for the commit phase.
